flashcards.front: Define the set of Whole Numbers (ℙ) in terms of Natural Numbers (ℕ).
flashcards.back: ℙ = ℕ ∪ {0} (the set of all non-negative integers).

flashcards.front: What algebraic property makes zero (0) unique under addition in the set of Whole Numbers?
flashcards.back: Zero is the additive identity: adding 0 to any whole number yields that number unchanged (a + 0 = a).

flashcards.front: Does every whole number have a predecessor in the set of Whole Numbers?
flashcards.back: No. The number 0 does not have a predecessor in ℙ because there are no whole numbers less than 0.
